# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Deno Deploy function for secure web scraping service

// Allowlist of permitted domains for fetching
const ALLOWED_DOMAINS: Set<string> = new Set([
  "api.github.com",
  "jsonplaceholder.typicode.com",
  "httpbin.org",
  "api.publicapis.org",
  "catfact.ninja",
  // Add other trusted domains as needed
]);

// Blocked IP patterns to prevent SSRF attacks against internal networks
const BLOCKED_IP_PATTERNS = [
  /^127\./,
  /^10\./,
  /^172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\./,
  /^192\.168\./,
  /^0\./,
  /^169\.254\./,
  /^::1$/,
  /^fc00:/,
  /^fe80:/,
  /^fd/,
  /^localhost$/i,
  /^metadata\.google\.internal$/i,
];

const MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB limit
const FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS = 10000; // 10 second timeout
const MAX_REDIRECTS = 3;

function isUrlSafe(urlString: string): { safe: boolean; reason?: string } {
  let parsedUrl: URL;

  try {
    parsedUrl = new URL(urlString);
  } catch {
    return { safe: false, reason: "Invalid URL format" };
  }

  // Only allow HTTPS
  if (parsedUrl.protocol !== "https:") {
    return { safe: false, reason: "Only HTTPS URLs are permitted" };
  }

  // Check against allowlist
  const hostname = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase();
  if (!ALLOWED_DOMAINS.has(hostname)) {
    return {
      safe: false,
      reason: `Domain '${hostname}' is not in the allowed domains list`,
    };
  }

  // Check for IP-based hostnames or blocked patterns
  for (const pattern of BLOCKED_IP_PATTERNS) {
    if (pattern.test(hostname)) {
      return { safe: false, reason: "Access to internal/private networks is forbidden" };
    }
  }

  // Reject URLs with credentials
  if (parsedUrl.username || parsedUrl.password) {
    return { safe: false, reason: "URLs with embedded credentials are not allowed" };
  }

  // Reject non-standard ports
  if (parsedUrl.port && parsedUrl.port !== "443") {
    return { safe: false, reason: "Only standard HTTPS port (443) is allowed" };
  }

  return { safe: true };
}

function sanitizeHeadersForResponse(headers: Headers): Record<string, string> {
  const safeHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};
  const allowedResponseHeaders = [
    "content-type",
    "content-length",
    "last-modified",
    "etag",
    "cache-control",
  ];

  for (const key of allowedResponseHeaders) {
    const value = headers.get(key);
    if (value) {
      safeHeaders[key] = value;
    }
  }

  return safeHeaders;
}

async function fetchWithSafety(
  url: string,
  signal: AbortSignal
): Promise<{ status: number; headers: Record<string, string>; body: string }> {
  let currentUrl = url;
  let redirectCount = 0;

  while (redirectCount <= MAX_REDIRECTS) {
    const validation = isUrlSafe(currentUrl);
    if (!validation.safe) {
      throw new Error(validation.reason);
    }

    const response = await fetch(currentUrl, {
      signal,
      redirect: "manual",
      headers: {
        "User-Agent": "SecureDataAggregator/1.0",
        Accept: "text/html, application/json, text/plain",
      },
    });

    // Handle redirects manually to validate each hop
    if ([301, 302, 303, 307, 308].includes(response.status)) {
      const location = response.headers.get("location");
      if (!location) {
        throw new Error("Redirect without Location header");
      }

      // Resolve relative URLs
      currentUrl = new URL(location, currentUrl).toString();
      redirectCount++;

      // Consume the body to free resources
      await response.body?.cancel();
      continue;
    }

    // Enforce response size limit
    const contentLength = response.headers.get("content-length");
    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) > MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE) {
      await response.body?.cancel();
      throw new Error(`Response too large: ${contentLength} bytes exceeds ${MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE} byte limit`);
    }

    // Read body with size limit
    const reader = response.body?.getReader();
    if (!reader) {
      return {
        status: response.status,
        headers: sanitizeHeadersForResponse(response.headers),
        body: "",
      };
    }

    const chunks: Uint8Array[] = [];
    let totalSize = 0;

    while (true) {
      const { done, value } = await reader.read();
      if (done) break;

      totalSize += value.byteLength;
      if (totalSize > MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE) {
        await reader.cancel();
        throw new Error(`Response body exceeded ${MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE} byte limit`);
      }

      chunks.push(value);
    }

    const combined = new Uint8Array(totalSize);
    let offset = 0;
    for (const chunk of chunks) {
      combined.set(chunk, offset);
      offset += chunk.byteLength;
    }

    const body = new TextDecoder().decode(combined);

    return {
      status: response.status,
      headers: sanitizeHeadersForResponse(response.headers),
      body,
    };
  }

  throw new Error(`Too many redirects (max ${MAX_REDIRECTS})`);
}

// Request only the specific permissions needed for allowed domains
async function requestNetworkPermissions(): Promise<void> {
  for (const domain of ALLOWED_DOMAINS) {
    try {
      await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net", host: domain });
    } catch {
      // On Deno Deploy, permissions are handled differently; continue gracefully
    }
  }
}

// Rate limiting (simple in-memory, per-IP)
const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetTime: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 30;

function checkRateLimit(clientIp: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(clientIp);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetTime) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientIp, { count: 1, resetTime: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return true;
  }

  if (entry.count >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) {
    return false;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return true;
}

// Clean up stale rate limit entries periodically
setInterval(() => {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [key, value] of rateLimitMap) {
    if (now > value.resetTime) {
      rateLimitMap.delete(key);
    }
  }
}, 60000);

// Initialize permissions at startup
await requestNetworkPermissions();

Deno.serve(async (request: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const corsHeaders = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
    "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
    "Strict-Transport-Security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains",
  };

  // Only allow GET requests
  if (request.method !== "GET") {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Method not allowed. Use GET." }),
      { status: 405, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }

  // Rate limiting by client IP
  const clientIp =
    request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
    request.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") ||
    "unknown";

  if (!checkRateLimit(clientIp)) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later." }),
      { status: 429, headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Retry-After": "60" } }
    );
  }

  const requestUrl = new URL(request.url);

  // Handle info endpoint
  if (requestUrl.pathname === "/info") {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        service: "Secure Data Aggregation Service",
        allowedDomains: [...ALLOWED_DOMAINS],
        usage: "GET /fetch?url=https://allowed-domain.com/path",
        limits: {
          maxResponseSize: `${MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE} bytes`,
          timeout: `${FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS}ms`,
          rateLimit: `${RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS} requests per minute`,
        },
      }),
      { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }

  // Only serve the /fetch endpoint
  if (requestUrl.pathname !== "/fetch") {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: "Not found. Use GET /fetch?url=<URL> or GET /info",
      }),
      { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }

  const targetUrl = requestUrl.searchParams.get("url");

  if (!targetUrl) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: "Missing 'url' query parameter",
        allowedDomains: [...ALLOWED_DOMAINS],
      }),
      { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }

  // Validate the URL before fetching
  const validation = isUrlSafe(targetUrl);
  if (!validation.safe) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: `URL validation failed: ${validation.reason}`,
        allowedDomains: [...ALLOWED_DOMAINS],
      }),
      { status: 403, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }

  // Fetch with timeout
  const abortController = new AbortController();
  const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => abortController.abort(), FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS);

  try {
    const result = await fetchWithSafety(targetUrl, abortController.signal);

    // Do not log the full URL or response body to avoid leaking sensitive data
    console.log(
      `Fetch completed: domain=${new URL(targetUrl).hostname}, status=${result.status}, size=${result.body.length}`
    );

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        success: true,
        fetchedUrl: targetUrl,
        status: result.status,
        headers: result.headers,
        bodyLength: result.body.length,
        body: result.body,
      }),
      { status: 200, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  } catch (error: unknown) {
    const message =
      error instanceof DOMException && error.name === "AbortError"
        ? "Request timed out"
        : error instanceof Error
          ? error.message
          : "Unknown error occurred";

    // Log error without sensitive details
    console.error(`Fetch error for domain ${new URL(targetUrl).hostname}: ${message}`);

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        success: false,
        error: message,
      }),
      { status: 502, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  } finally {
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
  }
});
